campaigns.title: Oil Consumption Inspection (Customer Satisfaction Notification)
campaigns.summary: 2015-2018 Jeep Renegade (FWD), 2016-2018 Fiat 500X, and 2015-2018 Ram ProMaster City with the 2.4L engine (sales codes ED6/ED8/EDD/EDE) may exhibit excessive oil consumption from low-tension piston rings, which can eventually cause a stall if unaddressed. The dealer procedure: top off oil, drive a monitored 1500-1700 miles, recheck the level after a 5-minute hot shutdown on level ground. Below the ADD mark = failed test -> long-block engine replacement.
campaigns.reference: NHTSA TSB doc MC-10220586-9999 (Customer Satisfaction Notification W80)

campaigns.title: PCM Reprogramming for Oil Consumption
campaigns.summary: A related Customer Satisfaction Notification issuing a PCM software update aimed at oil-consumption behavior on the same 2.4L engine family.
campaigns.reference: Customer Satisfaction Notification W84 (owner-reported; verify applicability by VIN with a dealer)

tsbs.title: MultiAir Actuator Assembly Diagnostic Procedure (2.4L Tigershark Only)
tsbs.summary: When diagnosing P-codes related to the MultiAir unit, the required check order is: (1) verify engine oil is not low, (2) verify the oil filter meets OEM spec, (3) verify oil is not dirty/deteriorated, (4) verify correct oil viscosity/weight, (5) verify oil is not contaminated (coolant/fuel), (6) verify the oil galley screen/filter feeding the MultiAir unit is not clogged — BEFORE replacing the MultiAir actuator itself.
